tasks.description: PACT Chamber 2 × Helixa — async trust session: JWT issuance, PROVISIONAL default, Helixa cred callback
tasks.note: Superseded: split into HELIXA-007A/B/C in week-112.json due to MiniMax truncation at line 95.

review.summary: Critical issue: pact-session.ts file is truncated mid-function, making it syntactically invalid. The cred-update route handler is incomplete.

issues.description: File is truncated at line 95 with incomplete code: 'const ses' - the cred-update route handler is cut off mid-variable declaration
Missing import for getHelixaTrustCeiling function that is imported but never used - dead code
In-memory session store will lose all data on process restart - acceptable for v1 but should be documented as limitation
Added JSDoc comments that weren't in the spec - minor deviation but acceptable

strengths: JWT implementation follows spec exactly with HMAC-SHA256 and no external deps
Ceiling resolution logic correctly implements Q1, Q5, and SIWA upgrade decisions
Async Helixa fetch pattern correctly implemented with no await
App.ts integration follows surgical edit pattern precisely
Error handling includes proper status codes and structured error responses

tasks.description: Invoice HTML download with tax line — GET /v1/invoices/:id/download renders tax_line from paymentDetails

review.summary: All three deliverables complete and correct. invoice-renderer.ts (87 lines) renders AMD-22 tax section conditionally. invoice-download.ts (51 lines) mounts GET /v1/invoices/:id/download. Route imported and mounted in app.ts. Zero TypeScript errors in new files. Initial REJECTED verdict was a false positive — swarm reviewer incorrectly flagged a truncation that did not exist on disk.
strengths: Clean separation of concerns between renderer and route handler
Follows TypeScript strict typing with proper interfaces
Conditional tax section rendering — zero overhead when no tax_line present
No auth required by design — invoice ID is the download credential
Correct mounting in app.ts following the specified order
